title: aconitum japonicum subsp. subcuneatum
intro: Aconitum japonicum subsp. subcuneatum, commonly known as the Japanese monk's hood or monk's hat, is a subspecies of the Aconitum japonicum plant, a type of flowering perennial plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic effects. The health benefits of Aconitum japonicum subsp. subcuneatum include its ability to relieve pain, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of various respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically dried and used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions, although it is essential to exercise caution when handling the plant due to its toxic properties.
